plan_metadata.name: group  debug in copilot remote DA no action Copilot License

steps.description: Press the F1 key to open the Command Palette in Visual Studio Code, within the context of the "Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit" workspace.
Type the text "debug: Select and Start Debugging" into the command palette search bar located at the top-center of the Visual Studio Code interface.
Press the "Enter" key to execute the selected command ("Debug: Select and Start Debugging") in the Visual Studio Code command palette.
Type the text 'Preview in Copilot (chrome)' into the dropdown input field located at the top of the Visual Studio Code interface within the debugging configuration menu.
Click the "Preview in Copilot (Chrome)" option from the dropdown menu in the debug configuration toolbar of the Visual Studio Code interface.
Click the red plus icon next to the "Email or phone" field in the Microsoft login form.
Enter ${{env:M365_ACCOUNT_NAME_EnableCopilotAccess}} into the "Email or phone" field on the Microsoft sign-in page in a Chrome browser.
Click the "Next" button on the Microsoft login screen after entering the email address, within the Chrome browser during the authentication workflow.
Type ${{secret:M365_ACCOUNT_PASSWORD_EnableCopilotAccess}} into the Password field on the Microsoft login page for user ${{M365.M365_ACCOUNT_PASSWORD_EnableCopilotAccess}}.
Press the "Enter" key after filling in the password field on the Microsoft login interface to proceed with authentication.
Click the "Yes" button on the "Stay signed in?" prompt within the Microsoft login interface at https://login.microsoftonline.com.
Perform keyboard shortcut "Ctrl+-" to reduce the zoom level in the web browser displaying the "Chat | M365 Copilot" interface.
Refresh the M365 Copilot web chat interface in Google Chrome by pressing the F5 key.
